ADP.reason: Stock is down ~25% over 1y yet earnings are expected to grow (fwd P/E below trailing), and a recent 3m rebound of +9% suggests the market may be recognizing a mispriced, stable payroll processor.

CCC.reason: Earnings are expected to grow, but the stock is down 45% over 1y and still falling (-16% in 3m), suggesting the market sees real competitive or structural risk despite the deep EV/EBITDA discount.

TEAM.reason: A 75% P/S discount is striking and the stock has rebounded +16% in 3m, but earnings direction is unclear and the 60% one-year drawdown signals the market is pricing material uncertainty.

ZTS.reason: Forward earnings are expected to grow, but a 52% one-year decline and continued 3m drop of -35% suggest the market is pricing a meaningful structural or regulatory concern beyond simple mean-reversion.

MMS.reason: A 53% P/E discount with earnings expected to grow and the stock still well off highs looks like an out-of-favor government services name primed for mean-reversion rather than structural impairment.

FDS.reason: Stock is down ~47% over 1y yet earnings are expected to grow and price has rebounded +18% in 3m, consistent with a stable data/analytics franchise simply out of favor.

ACN.reason: Earnings are expected to grow, but a 56% one-year decline with continued -34% in 3m suggests the market is pricing a prolonged consulting-demand downturn that may not reverse quickly.

DUOL.reason: The stock is down 69% over 1y and earnings are expected to FALL (fwd P/E above trailing), signaling the market is pricing deteriorating fundamentals rather than a temporary dislocation.

UHS.reason: A 47% P/E discount at 7.1x with earnings expected to grow suggests a stable hospital operator that is simply out of favor despite solid forward fundamentals.

CDW.reason: Stock is down 25% over 1y yet earnings are expected to grow and price has stabilized (+10% in 3m), consistent with a mean-reversion candidate in IT distribution.

ROP.reason: Earnings are expected to grow, but the stock is down 40% over 1y with no recent rebound (-4% in 3m), suggesting the market sees real risks to the compounder model despite the discount.

MKTX.reason: A 62% P/E discount is deep and earnings are expected to grow, but a 50% one-year decline and -32% in 3m suggest the market is pricing competitive pressures that may keep multiples compressed.

FMC.reason: Stock is down 72% over 1y with no rebound (-25% in 3m), earnings direction is unclear, and a 75% P/S discount suggests the market sees structural deterioration in the crop-protection business.

SPSC.reason: A 62% EV/EBITDA discount with earnings expected to grow and a recent +7% 3m rebound suggests a stable SaaS franchise simply out of favor rather than structurally impaired.

LTC.reason: Price is up 11% over 1y and near highs, but earnings are expected to FALL, suggesting the market is pricing a stable income name whose discount may reflect a rising-rate or sector headwind rather than deep distress.

SAIC.reason: A 49% P/E discount with earnings expected to grow, price up 3% over 1y and near highs, suggests a stable government IT services name trading cheaply without signs of structural deterioration.

KMB.reason: A modest 28% P/E discount with earnings expected to grow and price stabilizing (+11% in 3m) is consistent with a defensive staples name temporarily out of favor rather than a value trap.

TROW.reason: The stock is up 16% over 1y and near highs, but earnings are roughly flat and a 33% P/E discount may reflect structural pressure on active asset management fees that the market is correctly pricing.

AAP.reason: Price has rebounded strongly (+33% over 1y, +20% in 3m) and earnings are expected to grow, but the deep EV/EBITDA discount and prior collapse suggest the market still harbors concerns about the turnaround's durability.

G.reason: Earnings are expected to grow, but the stock is down 32% over 1y and still falling (-24% in 3m), suggesting the market sees cyclical or competitive headwinds in BPO beyond a simple dislocation.

DPZ.reason: Earnings are expected to grow, but the stock is down 34% over 1y and still declining (-17% in 3m), suggesting the market questions whether the growth model is decelerating despite the P/E discount.

OTIS.reason: A 21% P/E discount with earnings expected to grow and a relatively modest 24% one-year decline suggests a stable industrial franchise that is simply out of favor.

HLMN.reason: Price is up 19% over 1y, earnings are expected to grow, and the EV/EBITDA discount is deep, consistent with a small-cap industrials name gaining recognition rather than deteriorating.

LEG.reason: Earnings are expected to FALL (fwd P/E above trailing) and while price has rebounded, a 63% P/E discount at 6.2x suggests the market is pricing structural decline in the bedding/components business.

CRM.reason: Earnings are expected to grow, but the stock is down 41% over 1y and still declining (-15% in 3m), suggesting the market sees AI-driven competitive disruption that may cap multiple expansion despite the discount.

QLYS.reason: A 68% P/E discount with earnings expected to grow and a strong +35% 3m rebound suggests a stable cybersecurity name that was oversold and is mean-reverting.

FRSH.reason: Earnings are expected to grow and the stock has rebounded +10% in 3m, but a 41% one-year decline and 68% P/S discount suggest the market still questions the competitive position in a crowded SaaS market.

ARE.reason: A 70% P/S discount is extreme and price has rebounded +15% in 3m, but earnings direction is unclear and a 25% one-year decline suggest the market is pricing office/lab real-estate structural risk that may persist.

LULU.reason: The stock is down 49% over 1y and still declining (-22% in 3m) with earnings expected to FALL, signaling the market is pricing a genuine deceleration in the brand rather than a temporary dip.

JKHY.reason: Earnings are roughly flat and the stock is down 24% over 1y with no rebound (-14% in 3m), suggesting the market sees slowing growth in the core banking-tech franchise despite a 33% P/E discount.

WTW.reason: Earnings are expected to grow and the stock is only down 11% over 1y with a 41% P/E discount, consistent with a stable insurance broker that is simply out of favor rather than deteriorating.

CFFN.reason: Price is up 43% over 1y and near highs with earnings expected to grow, and the 41% P/E discount suggests a thrift that is mean-reverting and being recognized by the market.

ELV.reason: Price is up 6% over 1y with a strong +34% 3m rebound, earnings expected to grow, and a 26% P/E discount, all consistent with a stable health insurer that is out of favor and recovering.

OLLI.reason: Earnings are expected to grow, but the stock is down 45% over 1y and still declining (-21% in 3m), suggesting the market questions whether the off-price model is losing momentum despite the P/E discount.

PPG.reason: Price is up 9% over 1y with earnings expected to grow and a 37% P/E discount, consistent with a stable coatings leader that is simply out of favor rather than structurally impaired.

AVB.reason: Earnings are expected to FALL (fwd P/E above trailing) and the stock is only modestly down, suggesting the 27% P/E discount may reflect a correctly priced slowdown in apartment REIT fundamentals.

PSN.reason: Earnings are expected to grow, but the stock is down 26% over 1y with no rebound (-5% in 3m), suggesting the market sees budget or competitive pressures in the infrastructure business despite the discount.
